slides.title: The first thing to fade is your *stride*.
slides.sub: Walking speed quietly slows with age. Strong glutes and hip power are how you keep it.

slides.title: The posterior chain
slides.body: Your glutes, hamstrings and back drive every step. This *posterior chain* is the engine behind a strong, confident walk.

slides.title: Hip power moves you forward
slides.body: Hip extension is what propels you ahead with each stride. As it weakens, *gait and pace* are often the first to fade.

slides.title: Walking speed is a vital sign
slides.body: How fast you walk *is linked to* how long you stay independent. It is one of the simplest markers of aging well.

slides.title: Train hip extension
slides.body: Bridges, hip thrusts and step-ups build the glutes directly. A few sessions a week *can support* propulsive, steady walking.

slides.title: Then go use it
slides.body: Strength is the foundation. Daily walking turns it into real-world pace, so *use the legs* you are building.

slides.title: Keep the *stride* in your step.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps, movement, sleep and food, then turns it into one daily Health Score that keeps your training honest.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The first thing to slow down with age isn't your mind. It's your walk. 🌿

Strong glutes and hip power drive a propulsive stride, and walking speed is one of the clearest signs of aging well. Train the posterior chain, then go use it. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your steps, movement and habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Strong Glutes Keep You Walking Fast as You Age
